jobs.title: Full Stack Developer
jobs.descriptionHtml:
About School Prom Car Hire

School Prom Car Hire is a premium luxury transport and travel platform operating across the UK and internationally. We specialise in limousine hire, yacht charters, private aviation, chauffeur services, airport transfers, and high-end event transport.

Our growth strategy is built on technical excellence, search dominance, automation, and scalable digital infrastructure.

We are hiring a Senior Full-Stack Web Developer who understands how engineering decisions directly impact SEO performance, scalability, and long-term growth.

The Role

This position requires a true full-stack engineer — not just a WordPress developer.

You will take ownership of our entire digital ecosystem, ensuring it is technically sound, search-optimised, secure, automated, and built for algorithm resilience.

What You’ll Be Responsible For Full-Stack Development

  • Front-end and back-end development
  • Building scalable web applications
  • Working across multiple CMS platforms (not limited to WordPress)
  • Creating custom themes, modules, and integrations
  • API development and third-party integrations
  • Database optimisation and system performance tuning
  • Ensuring clean, modular, maintainable code

Technical SEO Architecture

  • Designing SEO-first site structures
  • Implementing Hub & Spoke content models
  • Building MCP (Modular Content Publishing) systems
  • Structured data and advanced schema deployment
  • Crawl optimisation and index control
  • Canonicalisation, redirect logic, hreflang implementation
  • Log file analysis
  • Core Web Vitals optimisation
  • Preparing infrastructure for Google Core Updates

You must understand modern search beyond basics — including semantic SEO, entity relationships, and algorithm volatility management.

Infrastructure & Performance

  • Server configuration (VPS / Cloud environments)
  • CDN setup and advanced caching systems
  • Security hardening and monitoring
  • Backup management and disaster recovery planning

CRM & AI Automation

  • CRM integration and optimisation
  • AI workflow implementation
  • Automation of operational and marketing systems
  • Process engineering to improve scalability

Requirements

  • 5+ years full-stack development experience
  • Strong front-end (HTML, CSS, JavaScript frameworks)
  • Strong back-end (PHP or equivalent server-side language)
  • Experience with multiple CMS ecosystems
  • Advanced technical SEO knowledge
  • Understanding of Google algorithm patterns and risk mitigation
  • Server and infrastructure management experience
  • Ability to manage multiple projects independently

Who This Role Is For

This is for someone who:

  • Thinks like an engineer and strategist
  • Understands growth, not just development
  • Anticipates algorithm shifts rather than reacting to them
  • Can build systems that scale internationally
  • Takes ownership and accountability

What We Offer

  • Competitive salary (based on experience)
  • Long-term strategic role
  • High-impact position within a scaling brand
  • Remote flexibility
  • Opportunity to lead technical direction

Application Process

Please submit:

  • CV
  • Portfolio or GitHub
  • Example of a full-stack system you’ve built
  • Example of technical SEO implementation
  • A short explanation of how you future-proof a site against algorithm changes

Job Types: Full-time, Part-time, Temporary, Freelance

Pay: From £1,500.00 per month

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Surbiton KT6 7NA

jobs.title: Workflow Developer
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Overview:
The Workflow Developer is responsible for designing, developing, and continually improving workflows, automations, and integrations across the firm’s core business systems — including case management and finance platforms. The role ensures that processes are efficient, reliable, and aligned with legal‑sector best practices.

Working closely with business stakeholders, the Workflow Developer translates operational needs into robust, secure, and well‑documented technical solutions that support high‑quality legal service delivery and contribute to the firm’s wider digital strategy.

The firm operates a hybrid agile working policy allowing employees to work from home (or another UK location) for up to 50% of their time. Individuals can also apply for other flexible working options either at the time of recruitment or during the course of their employment. The ability to occasionally travel to other firm locations for project work or system rollouts is desirable as well as participating in planned evening/weekend releases where required (with notice).
Responsibilities:

Workflow & Systems Engineering

  • Develop, configure, and maintain workflows across case management, practice management, and finance systems to support efficient legal and business operations.
  • Create SQL queries, stored procedures, scripts, views, and data routines to support workflow logic, reporting, and system optimisation.
  • Design integrations between systems using APIs, REST services, and approved integration tools.
  • Implement development standards, version control practices, and technical documentation aligned with firm‑wide governance.

Business Engagement & Requirements

  • Work closely with legal teams, Finance, and Business Services to gather and analyse requirements.
  • Translate business needs into clear technical specifications with documented acceptance criteria.
  • Communicate complex technical topics in a clear, structured, and accessible way to non‑technical users.
  • Support stakeholders through prototyping, demonstrations, user training, and change adoption.

Governance, Quality & Operational Excellence

  • Ensure workflow changes follow the firm’s change control and release processes.
  • Maintain accurate, audit‑ready documentation for all customisations, integrations, and data flows.
  • Participate in problem reviews and root‑cause analysis where workflows or data issues are involved.
  • Ensure solutions are secure, resilient, and compliant with legal‑sector data handling standards and GDPR.

Data, Automation & Integrations

  • Analyse end‑to‑end data flows across business systems and recommend improvements for performance, accuracy, and reliability.
  • Build and manage automations using integration platforms or low‑code tools (e.g., Power Automate, Boomi, or equivalent).
  • Optimise existing workflows and identify opportunities for innovation, simplification, or automation.

Projects, Testing & Release Management

  • Contribute to project planning, providing technical input on workflow impacts, effort estimates, and release sequencing.
  • Develop and execute test plans (unit, integration, UAT support) to ensure robust delivery.
  • Prepare and deliver handover documentation to IT Support, ensuring readiness for BAU operation.
  • Support rollout plans including communication, user guidance, and training coordination.

Third‑Party Coordination & Vendor Management

  • Collaborate with external suppliers on enhancements, defect resolution, updates, and technical troubleshooting.
  • Ensure supplier-delivered work complies with firm standards, development principles, and security requirements.
  • Monitor vendor performance and escalate issues where required.

Risk, Security & Compliance

  • Ensure workflows and system enhancements uphold confidentiality, GDPR, and legal services data governance standards.
  • Implement secure development practices including least‑privilege design, validated integrations, and audit‑ready logging.
  • Support Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery considerations for critical workflows.
Essential & Desirable Criteria:

We are looking for someone who is;

  • Customer‑centric, proactive in understanding user needs and improving system usability.
  • Structured, methodical approach with keen attention to detail and documentation quality.
  • Curious, innovative, and motivated to seek improvements and adopt modern tools.
  • Able to manage competing priorities and deliver against deadlines.
  • A strong team player who collaborates well with IT Support, Projects, Security, and business stakeholders.
  • Self‑motivated, adaptable, and comfortable working independently or in a dynamic team environment.

Essential

  • Minimum 2 years’ experience in a development, system customisation, or workflow-focused technical role.
  • Strong SQL experience (queries, stored procedures, performance troubleshooting).
  • Hands‑on experience designing workflows, automations, or system integrations.
  • Strong understanding of data flows and information architecture.
  • Ability to analyse complex requirements and produce structured, high‑quality technical solutions.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to engage confidently with stakeholders at all levels.
  • Experience creating and maintaining clear technical documentation.
  • Collaborative working style with strong problem‑solving capability.

Desirable

  • Experience in the legal sector or professional services environment.
  • Familiarity with systems such as iManage, Aderant, Peppermint, or similar case/practice management platforms.
  • Experience with integration platforms (e.g., Dell Boomi), automation tools (e.g., Power Automate), or low‑code development.
  • Understanding of ITIL principles (incident, change, problem).
  • Exposure to DevOps, CI/CD, or structured testing and release methodologies.
  • Knowledge of APIs, RESTful services, and authentication methods.

At our firm, Diversity, Equity and Inclusion is a priority and at the heart of everything we do. We actively want to attract a diverse workforce and welcome applications from everyone, from all backgrounds. We are committed to promoting an inclusive culture where everyone can be their full selves and experience being seen and heard. You can find out more about our firm’s commitment, initiatives and Pennclusion committees here.

We ensure that there are equal opportunities and treatment for all job applicants and employees, at all stages of the recruitment process and employment, regardless of age, gender reassignment, marriage or civil partnership, pregnancy and maternity, disability, race (including colour, nationality, ethnic or national origin), religion or belief, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression and social background. We aim to provide adjustments for people who have a disability, long-term health condition (including mental health) or neurodiversity. If you would like to request an adjustment, please contact Sam.Austin@penningtonslaw.com

jobs.companyDescription:
Penningtons Manches Cooper is a leading UK and international law firm which provides high quality legal advice tailored to both businesses and individuals. We address the specific objectives of each client through our flair and technical expertise and offer a personal service founded on a strong team ethic.



Today, we have UK offices in the City of London, Basingstoke, Birmingham, Cambridge, Guildford, Oxford and Reading while our overseas network stretches from Asia to South America through our presence in Singapore, Piraeus, Paris, Madrid and São Paulo. With 130 partners and over 880 people in total, our main areas of practice are dispute resolution, corporate and commercial, real estate, private client and family.



We have established a strong reputation in a variety of sectors, particularly private wealth, shipping, technology and property. Our lawyers are also recognised for their expertise in life sciences, education, retail, sports and entertainment and international trade. In these areas, we're acknowledged for our unparalleled market insight.



By joining forces with the partners and staff of Thomas Cooper in July 2019, we have added significant depth to our core practice. The move builds on the expansion of our litigation and arbitration offering and has introduced us to the maritime community in which Thomas Cooper has long been a key player.



Our continuing commitment to excellence is backed by a track record which dates back nearly 300 years. We pay great attention to matching your requirements with the right skills, delivered cost effectively and within the appropriate timescale.



Among our clients we count multi-national corporations, public companies, professional partnerships, banks and financial institutions as well as private individuals, owner managed businesses and start-ups. We are conscious that our culture and close working relationships are vital assets and we are determined to maintain these as we increase in size. 



Our broad international focus is supported by well-established links with law firms throughout the world. Penningtons Manches Cooper is a member of Multilaw and the European Law Group, networks with representatives in over 100 countries, and many of our lawyers play leading roles in various international bodies.

jobs.title: Analytics Developer
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Description


The purpose of this role is to assist Lancashire’s Advanced Analytics team in data management and insight into the Group’s modelled risk portfolio. Good Python programming and data manipulation skills using T-SQL are essential within this role.


Specific Responsibilities


  • Manage the data fidelity of the Analytics cube within Lancashire’s Azure Enterprise Data Warehouse.
  • Help support the Actuarial team with technical pricing in Python.
  • Work with the Analytics, EM and IT teams to seek platform efficiencies that may help to address gaps in the Analytics data.
  • Assist with optimization and analysis - apply quantitative techniques and models, coupled with specific market knowledge, to construct optimal structures and modelled portfolios.
  • Assist in the preparation of reports for Ratings Agency and Regulatory submissions.
  • Use SQL Server for extracting, importing, and updating submissions and risk modelling data.
  • Perform reconciliations to help identify any data anomalies.
  • Work with the Analytics team on additional projects e.g. research tasks and platform improvements outside of major renewal periods.
  • Any other duties as required.

Skills, Knowledge and Expertise


  • Bachelor’s degree in quantitative discipline e.g. Science/Maths/Computer Science.
  • A good understanding of Python programming essential and ability to use machine learning tools and statistical techniques to produce solutions to problems preferable.
  • Adept in data extraction and manipulation using T-SQL.
  • Some understanding of principles of implementing APIs via RESTful Web Services.
  • Experience working with catastrophe models, specifically RMS and AIR models and modelling workflow software such as ReMetrica, Igloo or similar would be helpful.
  • Familiarity with Power BI/Reporting Services would be advantageous.
  • Strong knowledge of Microsoft Excel (Access and VBA are also an advantage).
  • Excellent organisational and communication skills and ability to work independently in a team setting.

The Lancashire Way


At Lancashire, we believe our culture sets us apart. The way we behave and approach our work day-to-day is what makes us unique and creates a positive experience for our people, business partners and other stakeholders. Honesty and integrity in all we do is a given and The Lancashire Way reflects our true character and spirit.

Straight-talking
We feel empowered to share thoughts and ideas, because everyone’s voice matters.
Collaborative
We work together towards common goals, share knowledge and support each other.
Hard-working
We all have a stake in the company’s success and are proactive in contributing to our goals and vision.
Responsible
We focus on achieving tangible results with consistent standards across the Group.

Positive
We engage with brokers, clients, communities, stakeholders and colleagues professionally and passionately as proud ambassadors of Lancashire.


About The Lancashire Group

Lancashire are a provider of global specialty insurance and reinsurance products operating in Bermuda, London, the U.S. and Australia, across three delivery platforms: rated company, Lloyd’s and collateralised security.
Our focus is on short-tail, specialty (re)insurance risks within four general segments: Property and Casualty, Aviation, Energy and Marine, and Specialty.

jobs.title: Senior Platform Engineer
jobs.descriptionHtml:
At Lunar Energy, we're on a mission to transform the way we power our homes by building an ecosystem of all-electric products, starting with a next-generation home battery system and a cloud PaaS to manage large fleets of Distributed Energy Resources (DER) assets. Comprising a team of visionary entrepreneurs and dedicated technology and industry experts, we're united by our shared vision of deploying and building products to electrify homes that are connected through an integrated software platform.

We are currently seeking a Senior Platform Engineer to join our talented team of engineers, dedicated to delivering a more sustainable future. You will play a pivotal role in ensuring end-to-end platform reliability, security, and internal tooling. You will have the opportunity to strongly influence and contribute to the team's future.

Lunar Energy empowers you to explore multiple facets of our technology stack, aligned with your personal interests and abilities. We have a strong tradition of leveraging functional programming and type systems to craft pragmatic and flexible systems that align with business requirements.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Design and implement scalable, highly available infrastructure and internal tooling solutions using TypeScript and Python in AWS
  • Enable fast iteration through implementation and improvement of automation
  • Take ownership of ensuring continuous operation of critical systems, including databases and ETL pipelines
  • Reason about cloud infrastructure strategy to help ensure that solutions are the best fit for requirements
  • Develop and evangelise security best practices, such as automation of a secure software development process

Key Qualifications:

  • Bachelor's or Master's degree in a technical field, or equivalent practical experience.
  • A minimum of 5 years' of experience in DevOps, SecOps or Platform Engineering roles.
  • Experience building CI/CD pipelines
  • Experience using infrastructure as code
  • Experience managing cloud databases at scale in at least one of: Postgresql, Elasticsearch, InfluxDB
  • Hands-on experience with AWS
  • Strong understanding of networking and network security
  • Proven industry experience in Secops
  • Proficiency in one or more scripting programming languages (Python, Ruby, Bash, etc.)
  • Linux

Desirable Skills:

  • Experience with serverless technologies
  • Experience with Typescript
  • Proficiency in application penetration testing, ethical hacking, and vulnerability assessments
  • Interest in the IoT and renewable energy industry

You Are:

  • Impact-Driven: Thrive in a small, agile team where your initiative and contributions make a significant impact.
  • Curious and Enthusiastic: Eager to embrace new challenges and continuously expand your skill set.
  • Self-Starter: Capable of managing your own workload and deadlines while taking personal responsibility for your tasks.

Joining us gives you:

  • A competitive salary and stock options package
  • 25 days annual leave a year + public holidays
  • Private Medical Insurance via BUPA (medical history disregarded)
  • A wellbeing and lifestyle benefits plan through Medicash
  • 5% employer contributions pension matching
  • A budget for work-related training of £1,000 per financial year and an allowance of 4 training days
  • Cycle To Work scheme and Workplace Nursery benefit
  • Access to Spill - Mental Health Support platform
  • Enhanced Maternity + Paternity Pay
  • Regular socials and activities, online and in-person
  • Flexible start and finish times, fully remote working or hybrid depending on preferences

Duration: Full-Time

Location: Fully remote within the UK, but you are welcome any time at our office in Spitalfields, London (UK), as often as you like.

We are proud to be an equal opportunities employer and welcome applications from all suitably qualified persons regardless of race, sex, ethnicity, disability, religion/belief, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, nationality, age or social background. We would like to actively encourage applications from those who identify with less represented and minority groups. We do not filter applications by university background and encourage those who have taken alternative educational and career paths to apply.
